Liivv Well

Support at every stage

We get it—healthcare isn't simple.

Life changes, your needs change, and we're here to support you every step of the way.

Got Ostomy?

Whether you have a new stoma, or you're a seasoned ostomy veteran; managing your ostomy can feel overwhelming.

Whether you’re looking for products, treatment, or everyday wellness support, Liivv is here to make self‑care feel easier, more accessible, and a little more human.

Your First 30 Days, Simp(liivv)fied

Transitioning from hospital to home can feel overwhelming.

We’ve broken down the essentials into clear, step-by-step guidance so you can focus on healing, adapting, and finding your new normal with confidence.

Curated Essentials for Every Body

Lifestyle, Connection, and Support

Explore honest conversations about the realities of living with a stoma. Dive into expert advice on diet, mental health, intimacy, and fitness, and discover financial resources and peer support networks across Canada.

Every Day Living

Meet Mya

You Are Not Alone...

"My name is Mya, and I was diagnosed with severe Ulcerative Colitis when I was 8 years old. I spent the majority of my childhood roaming the halls of Sick Kids Hospital. While other kids worried about school dances, first crushes, or homework, I was counting bathroom trips and hiding pain nobody else could see. I became too familiar with hospital foods, IV lines, and the act of pretending everything was fine..."

Sometimes the best resource is a conversation. Connect with a peer who has walked the path before you.

Read Her Full Story Find A Peer Support Group

Support, Wherever You Are

Access funding, programs, and resources available across Canada

🧠 What to Expect

Coverage varies widely by province

Some programs:

  • give fixed yearly payments
  • others cover a percentage
  • some offer full coverage
✨ How to Use This

If you’re just getting started:

  1. Find your province
  2. Apply for your main program
  3. Check if you qualify for additional support
  4. Ask an ET nurse for help navigating options
Canada-Wide Programs

💰 Government Tax Credits

Disability Tax Credit (DTC)

  • Reduces income tax for people with long-term medical needs
  • Can unlock additional benefits like savings plans
  • Apply for Disability Tax Credit

🧰 Federal Coverage Programs

Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB)

  • Covers ostomy supplies for eligible First Nations and Inuit
  • Includes medical supplies and equipment
  • NIHB medical supplies coverage

🤝 Community Support

Ostomy Canada Society

  • Support groups across Canada
  • Education and resources
  • Help navigating funding
Ontario

💰 Assistive Devices Program (ADP)

  • ~$975 per year per ostomy
  • Up to ~$1300 with additional support

How to apply:

  1. See your doctor or nurse practitioner
  2. Complete the ADP form
  3. Submit to the program

👉 Official info: Ontario ADP ostomy funding

➕ Additional Help

  • Ontario Works / ODSP may increase coverage
  • Payments are made directly to you
British Columbia

💰 PharmaCare (Fair PharmaCare)

  • Helps cover ostomy supplies based on income
  • Coverage increases as your income decreases

👉 Learn more:

  • BC PharmaCare program details
Alberta

💰 Alberta Aids to Daily Living (AADL)

  • Covers most ostomy supply costs
  • You pay up to 25% (max $500/year), then full coverage

👉 Learn more:

  • AADL ostomy coverage
Quebec

Quebec

💰 Ostomy Appliances Program (RAMQ)

  • Annual funding:
    ~$1,510 (permanent ostomy)
    ~$1,007 (temporary)

👉 Apply here:

  • RAMQ ostomy program
Manitoba

💰 Manitoba Ostomy Program (MOP)

  • Full coverage for eligible residents

How it works:

  • Register through an ET nurse
  • Supplies are shipped directly
Saskatchewan

💰 SAIL Program

  • Covers ~50% of ostomy supplies
  • 100% coverage in some cases

👉 Learn more:

  • SAIL ostomy program
New Brunswick

💰 Ostomy / Incontinence Program

  • Covers supplies for eligible social development clients
  • No cost if you qualify

👉 Learn more:

  • New Brunswick ostomy program
Nova Scotia

💰 Pharmacare + Support Programs

  • Can provide 100% coverage depending on eligibility
  • Income-based programs available

👉 Learn more:

  • Nova Scotia ostomy coverage

We Thought You Might Ask

Do I need a diagnosis to shop here?

Not at all. Many people come here simply to explore everyday care products or support what they’re already experiencing. You don’t need a diagnosis, a label, or a specific reason to be here — just curiosity or a desire to feel supported.

I’m new to this — where should I begin?

Start simple. A few reliable essentials and a basic routine are all you need to begin.
You can always adjust as you figure out what works best for you.

How do I know if I’m using the right products?

If things feel secure, comfortable, and easy to manage, you’re on the right track.
If not, small changes in fit or product type can make a big difference.

How can I keep my skin healthy?

Keeping the area clean, dry, and protected is key. A good seal and the right barrier products make a big difference over time.

Can I live a normal, active life with an ostomy?

Yes. With the right setup, most people return to their regular routines—whether that’s work, exercise, travel, or everyday activities.

Do I need special cleaning products?

Not always, but products like adhesive removers and cleansing wipes can make the process easier and more comfortable.

Do you offer prescriptions or professional support?

If you’re looking for more support, we do offer access to consultations, treatments, and prescriptions where appropriate. These are always optional and handled with care, privacy, and respect.

Can I talk to someone if I’m not sure what I need?

Absolutely. If you have questions, want reassurance, or just need help choosing between a few options, our care team is here. There’s no pressure to know what to ask — we’ll meet you where you are.

What if I’m not sure what I need?

That’s completely okay. Start with the basics and take it step by step.
You’ll learn what works for you, and we’re here to help along the way.

We’re Here if You Need Us

If you have questions — or want a second opinion — we’re always here.
